title: Java PathFinder Symbolic Execution Pipeline
summary: A three-paper chain implementing Java program verification via model checking and symbolic execution. Paper 1 (JPF Core) implements the base Java model checker with state-space exploration, backtracking, and property checking. Paper 2 (Symbolic PathFinder) extends JPF with symbolic execution capabilities using custom bytecode interpretation, path condition management, and constraint solving. Paper 3 (ShadowJPF+) adds shadow symbolic execution for regression testing, combining complete symbolic execution with four-way forking to detect behavioral divergences between program versions.
instructionPreview: Implement a Java program verification toolset based on model checking and symbolic execution. The workspace contains a multi-project structure (jpf-core, jpf-symbc, jpf-shadow-plus, SootConnection) with Ant build files, external solver libraries (Z3, Choco, etc.), and example programs.
instruction:
Implement a Java program verification toolset based on model checking and symbolic execution.
The workspace contains a multi-project structure (jpf-core, jpf-symbc, jpf-shadow-plus, SootConnection)
with Ant build files, external solver libraries (Z3, Choco, etc.), and example programs.

You must implement three modules in topological order:
1. jpf_core: The core JPF model checker (custom JVM, state management, DFS search, property checking)
2. symbolic_pathfinder: Symbolic execution engine (SymbolicInstructionFactory, path conditions, solver integration)
3. shadow_jpf_plus: Shadow symbolic execution for regression testing (DiffExpression, four-way forking)

Each module includes a run.sh that builds, runs experiments, and outputs results as EXPERIMENT_NAME: VALUE.
Tests validate experiment outputs are within tolerance of expected values.

Key function signatures:
- gov.nasa.jpf.JPF: main orchestrator (Config, VM, Search, Reporter)
- gov.nasa.jpf.symbc.SymbolicInstructionFactory: custom bytecode factory for symbolic execution
- gov.nasa.jpf.shadow.ShadowInstructionFactory: extends SymbolicInstructionFactory for shadow execution
- gov.nasa.jpf.shadow.RunnerShadowPlus: experiment driver for shadow symbolic execution

moduleDag.description: A three-paper chain implementing Java program verification via model checking and symbolic execution. Paper 1 (JPF Core) implements the base Java model checker with state-space exploration, backtracking, and property checking. Paper 2 (Symbolic PathFinder) extends JPF with symbolic execution capabilities using custom bytecode interpretation, path condition management, and constraint solving. Paper 3 (ShadowJPF+) adds shadow symbolic execution for regression testing, combining complete symbolic execution with four-way forking to detect behavioral divergences between program versions.

nodes.label: Java PathFinder: A System to Verify Java Programs
nodes.description: Core JPF model checker implementing a custom JVM with state management, backtracking, depth-first search, choice generators for non-deterministic scheduling, property checking (deadlock, uncaught exceptions), and configuration-driven architecture.

nodes.label: Symbolic PathFinder: Symbolic Execution of Java Bytecode
nodes.description: Symbolic execution engine extending JPF via custom InstructionFactory. Implements symbolic bytecode interpretation, path condition management, PCChoiceGenerator for branching, constraint solver integration (Choco, Z3, Coral), a priori satisfiability optimization, and symbolic execution tree extension.

nodes.label: ShadowJPF+: Shadow Symbolic Execution with Java PathFinder
nodes.description: Shadow symbolic execution for regression testing. Extends SPF with DiffExpression (shadow+symbolic value pairs), four-way forking (same_true/same_false/diff_true/diff_false), ShadowPCChoiceGenerator, directed exploration via control-flow analysis, and change()-annotation handling for unified old/new program versions.

edges.label: SPF extends JPF's InstructionFactory and VM for symbolic execution
ShadowJPF+ extends SPF with shadow expressions and four-way forking
